title: Investigation of mindfulness meditation practitioners with voxel-based morphometry
headline: Investigation of mindfulness meditation practitioners with voxel-based morphometry
summary: A mechanistic/observational study of Vipassana examined brain & mechanisms. Both regions have previously been found to be involved in meditation. Results suggest that meditation practice is associated with structural differences in regions that are typically activated during meditation and in regions that are relevant for the task of meditation.
keyFindings: Both regions have previously been found to be involved in meditation.

Results suggest that meditation practice is associated with structural differences in regions that are typically activated during meditation and in regions that are relevant for the task of meditation.
limitations: Observational/mechanistic design cannot establish that the practice caused the observed differences.
The practice studied (Vipassana) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.

Results suggest that meditation practice is associated with structural differences in regions that are typically activated during meditation and in regions that are relevant for the task of meditation.
